discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape with mega-cap tech leading (MSFT +5.4%, ORCL +10.8%, AVGO +4.7% on Dell AI server readthrough), while energy, staples and discretionary lag as oil posts its biggest monthly loss in six years on prospective Iran deal. VIX down to 15.3 — risk-on under the hood despite IWM weakness. Mid-cap action thin; BBY post-earnings extension and AMR coal pullback are the cleanest setups.

notable_index_moves: XLK +2.2% driven by ORCL/MSFT/AVGO AI-infra bid following Dell's 32% earnings move
XLE -1.2% and XLP -1.8% as Iran de-escalation narrative crushes oil and defensives
IWM -0.55% diverging from SPY/QQQ — small-caps not participating in the AI bid
VIX -2.7% to 15.3, consistent with Iran-deal optimism

discoveries.name: Best Buy Co., Inc.
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings continuation extending a multi-day run-up in a weak discretionary tape
discoveries.thesis: BBY printed earnings (8-K 2.02 on 5/28) and has now extended sharply over the trailing week while XLY is red on the day. A retailer with structural CE-demand headwinds and tariff exposure rallying this hard into a soft consumer tape suggests squeeze/positioning dynamics layered on a genuine beat — the magnitude of the multi-day move likely overshoots the fundamental delta. Fade candidates here typically retrace 20-30% of the post-print run.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side notes flagging that guide was only modestly raised, short-interest data showing meaningful covering, or signs the move stalls on heavy volume without further upside news
discoveries.what_kills: The 8-K transcript reveals a major comp-sales acceleration or AI-PC refresh cycle commentary that justifies a structural rerate; continued sector-wide retail strength
discoveries.what_to_learn: Multi-day post-earnings extensions in weak sector tape are higher-probability fade setups than single-day prints — the longer the run on no new news, the more positioning-driven it becomes
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K item 2.02 confirms earnings as the catalyst; magnitude of trailing-week move alongside XLY weakness today implies positioning excess on top of the print
research_pointers: Pull the actual Q1 release: comp-sales number, guide, gross margin trajectory
Check short interest and days-to-cover going into the print
Compare to peer reactions (DKS, AAP, BBWI) to isolate company-specific vs. retail rotation
Tariff exposure commentary on CE imports given current administration stance

discoveries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
discoveries.setup: Commodity-linked name giving back part of a sharp weekly run-up as oil/energy complex sells off
discoveries.thesis: AMR ran hard into today and is pulling back as XLE drops on Iran-deal optimism. Met coal isn't oil but trades with broader commodity risk sentiment, and a give-back after a sharp run on no company-specific 8-K is the expected behavior. Magnitude looks proportionate — no directional edge.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued energy/commodity weakness Monday, no AMR-specific news appearing over weekend
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K appears (contract, buyback, M&A) suggesting today's selloff was actually fundamental
discoveries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; profit-taking after weekly run alongside energy complex weakness on Iran de-escalation
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, XLE -1.16% same day, AMR had run sharply the prior week
research_pointers: Met coal benchmark pricing trend (distinct from thermal/oil)
Any China steel demand data points this week
Confirm no 8-K filed after market close

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Semicap-adjacent name selling off on heavy volume with no 8-K and a flat-to-down SMH tape
discoveries.thesis: AEIS is down with elevated volume but no filing, no earnings within window, and the broader semi tape (SMH -0.15%, NVDA -1.45%) doesn't fully explain the magnitude. Could be a sell-side downgrade, channel check, or sympathy with a specific customer — without that data point I can't articulate either side at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: Discovery of a downgrade, negative channel check on Applied Materials/Lam, or customer-specific concentration warning
discoveries.what_kills: News of a positive event being faded for unrelated reasons, or confirmation this is just position unwinding
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; volume 2.1x avg suggests institutional flow but source not visible
research_pointers: Check for sell-side rating changes today on AEIS
Look at MKSI, AMAT comments — semicap subsector behavior
Review last earnings call for customer concentration risk (LRCX, AMAT exposure)

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates
discoveries.setup: Application software name participating in the broad XLK +2.2% AI-infra rally
discoveries.thesis: MANH is up on no specific catalyst, alongside a sharp XLK move driven by ORCL/MSFT/AVGO. Supply-chain software has tangential AI/agentic narrative pull. Magnitude looks consistent with sector beta plus mild momentum chase — no edge either way.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sector-wide app-software strength continues without MANH-specific news
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K or contract announcement appears suggesting today was fundamental, not beta
discoveries.catalyst: Sector rotation into software/AI-adjacent names on XLK strength
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, XLK +2.23%, mid-cap software typically betas to mega-cap moves
research_pointers: Check if any analyst day or conference presentation scheduled
Compare MANH move to other supply-chain software peers (SPS, DSGX)

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Dell prints +32% on AI server revenue +757% YoY; reads through to AI infrastructure supply chain

secondary_opportunities.relationship: AI cloud capex peer benefiting from same narrative
Direct AI server competitor — mid-cap readthrough candidate

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Trump signals Iran deal framework; Hormuz to reopen, naval blockade lifted; Brent posts biggest monthly loss in 6 years

secondary_opportunities.relationship: Defensive rotation reverses on risk-on de-escalation

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump posts detailed Iran framework: Hormuz reopens, naval blockade lifted, nuclear enriched material to be destroyed jointly with US/China/IAEA. Final determination pending Situation Room meeting.
trump_signals.note: Specific operational language (mines, ships heading home, no money exchanged) is more concrete than prior posts — market is pricing de-escalation (Brent biggest monthly drop in 6 years, VIX -2.7%). Risk: deal collapses in Situation Room, reverses oil and risk assets sharply. Defense names could fade on de-escalation but historically rally on regional instability — watch for reversal there.

trump_signals.post_summary: Reference to US-EU trade deal needing enforcement (Puzder Politico interview)
trump_signals.note: Reminder that EU trade-deal compliance remains a live policy lever; if specifics emerge could pressure European-exposed multinationals or tariff-sensitive sectors

discovery.no_signals_note: Mid-cap mover list was thin — only 4 candidates, of which 2 had no 8-K and only BBY had clear catalyst documentation. The real action today is in the Dell/AI-infra catalyst chain (mega-cap) and the Iran-deal commodity unwind, neither of which is a mid-cap discovery per se.

ai_announcements.headline: Glean's top line crosses $300M as AI budget cutting becomes its major selling point
ai_announcements.summary: Enterprise AI search startup Glean tripled annual revenue, positioned as cost-cutting tool against incumbent enterprise software.

ai_announcements.headline: Companies becoming 'too AI-pilled' - ClickUp cuts 22% workforce for AI agents
ai_announcements.summary: Discussion of accelerating tech layoffs in 2026 due to AI agent adoption, with Box's Aaron Levie calling out 'AI psychosis' among execs.

ai_announcements.headline: Groq raising $650M; XCENA raises $135M on memory bottleneck thesis
ai_announcements.summary: Continued capital flow into AI inference chips and memory startups. Groq pivoting from hardware toward inference focus.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: AEIS sells precision power conversion for semiconductor capex and data centers - AI buildout is a tailwind, not a threat. Today's -4.7% on 2.13x volume is more likely tied to semi-cap weakness or specific guidance/peer read-through than to Groq/XCENA news.
research_pointers: Check for AEIS-specific news, analyst downgrades, or semi-cap peer moves (AMAT, LRCX)
Confirm no guide-down; AEIS data center power exposure is structurally long AI
5-day -6.65% suggests broader semi-equipment selloff, not AI-displacement story

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's news flow is light on market-moving AI announcements. The notable stock movers (AMR coal, BBY retail earnings, MANH software) have no clear linkage to today's AI headlines. MANH +4.8% is interesting given supply-chain software is often cited as AI-vulnerable, but no specific catalyst in the news set. BBY move is explained by 8-K earnings filing. AMR is commodity-driven. AEIS weakness flagged but low-confidence AI attribution.
